author: @martynharveythepoet5114
text: Hi Rob, I realise that this video is a couple of years out of date but, as a newcomer to your channel, I thought I'd just offer my genuine response: Your channel is an amazing work of art - celebrating the never-ending talent and ingenuity that is the underlying theme of our human, industrial and intellectual development. Unfortunately, as a viewer, I see this demo as a thinly-veiled attempt by a third party to promote the standard petro-chemical "engine" by way of making it attractive to model-makers... Hmmm, feels like someone in the petro-chemical industry wants to leverage the following you have to draw attention to the old petrol/diesel engine. I know your viewers won't be foxed... there is much better energy to be obtained from Earth Batteries, Wind Turbines etc etc... than the energy obtained by reconstructing decades-old engines born of an era when cheap fossil fuels were everywhere. Another very well presented video but... those of us interested in future sustainable energy will probably not be entertained by this model. Come on dude... stay where your heart is - Free Energy! (and sustainability!) :-)

author: @sofa-lofa4241
text:
Very nice! It may seem a bit pricey to some but I used to work for an investment casting and injection moulding company and there is a lot of work put into the production of these kits, the fettling and anodising looks very good, 
Very nice packaging too... I wonder what the tin and plastic sheets will be used for, lol 🤔

P. S. They have a new year's 10% off deal now and it's nice to see they put up your video unedited on the product page

Great miniature engine model the finishing was Truely amazing, those two blue plastics will surely serve as good seperators and a dielectric in building some capacitors. If that same engine was running on fuel we could have utilised it to run a alternator in an ev to save one from a dead battery atleast able to charge it 20% on a liter of fuel, that would have been just too satisfying to experience!! 😂😂 Love and very warm regards to you Rob Sir from India 🇮🇳

author: @timcollins3484
text: The details at the front of the assembly manual would be great for younger people? Despite the information on the sites heading saying "adults," this is something I would have been OK to complete when i was 10. And with the demise of ICE engines caused by electric motors the model would be very collectible? At least it would be a nice, unbreakable  and very interesting display piece.

author: @jasonwitt8619
text:
Oh WOW mate, they have some very nice products, my grandson would love some of those robots and or car builds that they have. I like that they are all metal builds, I guess I will get him one or two for Christmas. Great job on showing this off mate, it sold me just by looking at what they sale.
PS, it might be a good idea to reach out to a couple of these Neo magnet companies and see if they want to trade advertisement for product, they could send you a 60 of 3/4" x 3/4" N52s that could be used on a lot of your projects.

author: @NorthernKitty
text: That's a site I've looked at many times and been tempted to purchase from - they have so many "steampunk" type of items!!  They have a transmission gear clock I've been musing over, and a pendulum clock that's a little out of my entertainment budget.   If there are "no strings attached" and it fits your content, I say "go for it!!" when you're offered to review or unbox products.  No shame in it at all, and I'm sure a great number of us are always looking for quality ideas!!  😊  Thanks for this, Rob, just in time for holiday gift buying!!  I'm sure a few of us just added an entry to our "Wish List". 😋
